diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional presyncope from cerebral hypoperfusion is a hallmark of symptomatic bradyarrhythmia/conduction disease.
Ischaemia can cause presyncope through arrhythmia or reduced output.
Exertional presyncope is a classic feature of severe aortic stenosis.
Exertional central chest heaviness in an older hypertensive patient is possible angina/ACS until excluded.
Exertional chest heaviness (angina) is one of the cardinal aortic stenosis symptoms.
Exertional breathlessness is a common anginal equivalent.
Non-specific; PE is less likely with normal oxygen saturation and no pleuritic pain.
Chronic low output from developing conduction disease can present as fatigue.
Non-specific; anaemia is a reasonable alternative cause of a month of fatigue and exertional breathlessness.
A positional element is present, but exertional chest heaviness makes a purely benign reflex cause unsafe to assume.

symptoms.symptom_name: No palpitations, no frank syncope, no orthopnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of palpitations makes a primary sustained tachyarrhythmia less likely, but does not exclude intermittent bradyarrhythmia.
Normal resting oxygen saturation makes a large PE less likely, though it does not exclude it. Patient-obtainable, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fixed, widely split second heart sound on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bundle branch block / intraventricular conduction delay confirmed on ECG
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fixed wide split S2 reflects delayed ventricular activation from a bundle branch block; sealed clinician auscultatory finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG: QRS >120 ms with rsR' in V1 (RBBB) or broad R in lateral leads (LBBB)

investigation_details.expected_result: Widened QRS >120 ms with a bundle branch block morphology; assess for AV block and ischaemic changes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The conduction abnormality and any high-grade AV block or acute ischaemia go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bundle branch block / intraventricular conduction delay confirmed on ECG
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ECG is the defining test for bundle branch block and screens simultaneously for AV block and ischaemia.

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — used to confirm or exclude acute myocardial injury
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Acute coronary syndrome driving the presentation and the new conduction block would be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Troponin with serial ECG is required to confirm or exclude acute myocardial ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No ejection systolic murmur radiating to the carotids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the characteristic murmur reduces the likelihood of significant aortic stenosis, but auscultation cannot be performed via telehealth so it cannot be relied upon remotely.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction disease / possible acute coronary syndrome
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional presyncope, chest heaviness and dyspnoea together in an older adult constitute a time-critical cardiac presentation requiring emergency assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the conduction abnormality and screens for high-grade AV block and acute ischaemia — must be done urgently in a monitored setting.
Confirms or excludes acute myocardial injury driving the presentation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods including full blood count and electrolytes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess anaemia and electrolyte disturbance as contributors to fatigue and conduction disturbance.
